basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Ibrāhīm — sūra of the Patriarch — opens on the Book sent down to bring mankind from darkness into light, by their Lord's permission. Qushayrī unfolds this “bringing forth” into a cascade: from the darknesses of ignorance into the light of knowledge, from doubt into certainty, from calculation into the contemplation of the Decree, from innovation into following, from the soul's claims into the heart's knowings, from separation into union. The sūra then recounts Moses and his people, and the resistance of the nations to their messengers — “can there be doubt about God, Originator of the heavens and the earth?” It unfolds the great parable: the good word as a good tree, root firm and branches in the sky, yielding fruit at every season; and the bad word as an uprooted tree, with no foundation. Qushayrī makes of this tree the kalimat al-tawḥīd: its trunk is knowledge, its branches the works, its leaves the courtesies of servitude, its flowers the beautiful virtues, its fruits the sweetness of obedience. Then comes Abraham's prayer — the model of divine friendship (khulla) — for Mecca, for his offspring settled in the barren valley, for the hearts that will incline toward them; and the praise: “Praise be to God who has granted me, in old age, Ishmael and Isaac.” “If you tried to count God's favors, you could not number them.” The sūra is sealed: this is a Message for mankind, that they may know “He is One God.”
themes: the messengers and the peoples' resistance
the tree of the good word
the Message of the One God

versets.ar: الر ۚ كِتَابٌ أَنْزَلْنَاهُ إِلَيْكَ لِتُخْرِجَ النَّاسَ مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَاطِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَمِيدِ
versets.tr: alif lām rā kitābun anzalnāhu ilayka li-tukhrija n-nāsa mina ẓ-ẓulumāti ilā n-nūri bi-idhni rabbihim ilā ṣirāṭi l-ʿazīzi l-ḥamīdi
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Rā. This is a Book We have sent down upon you that you may bring forth mankind from the darknesses into the light — by the permission of their Lord — toward the path of the Mighty, the Praiseworthy:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī swears by these letters: it is a Book sent down upon you that you may bring mankind by it from the darknesses of ignorance into the light of knowledge; from the darknesses of doubt into the light of certainty; from the darknesses of calculation into the open space of contemplating the Decree; from the darknesses of innovation into the light of following; from the darknesses of the soul's claims into the light of the heart's knowings; from the darknesses of separation into the light of union. All this by the permission of their Lord, by His prior will — toward the path of His mercy, the way of tawḥīd and the witnessings of unification.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ن ز ل
mots.sens: a Book We sent down to you

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ن و س
ظ ل م / ن و ر
ص ر ط / ع ز ز / ح م د
mots.sens: to the path of the Mighty, the Praiseworthy

versets.ar: اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۗ وَوَيْلٌ لِلْكَافِرِينَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: allāhi lladhī lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-waylun lil-kāfirīna min ʿadhābin shadīdin
versets.trad: God, to whom belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth. And woe to the disbelievers, who await a severe punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made creation know that God is the One to whom belongs all that is in the heavens and on the earth. Whoever knows Him has the praiseworthy return; whoever denies Him has the severe punishment — and that punishment is his very ignorance, glory to Him, of Who He is. To know is already to enter into praise; to deny is to lock oneself in the night that will become one's torment.

mots.racine: أ ل ه

mots.ar: مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍ
mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: what is in the heavens and earth

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ع ذ ب / ش د د

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَسْتَحِبُّونَ الْحَيَاةَ الدُّنْيَا عَلَى الْآخِرَةِ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَيَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ فِي ضَلَالٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: alladhīna yastaḥibbūna l-ḥayāta d-dunyā ʿalā l-ākhirati wa-yaṣuddūna ʿan sabīli llāhi wa-yabghūnahā ʿiwajan ulāʾika fī ḍalālin baʿīdin
versets.trad: Those who prefer the life of this world to the Hereafter, who turn people away from the path of God and seek to make it crooked — those are in profound error.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī details their portrait: they are those who prefer the rubbish of this world to the dignity of the bounties of the Hereafter — through the very hardness of their denial — and they seek to bend the religion by multiplying attachments. For such, in this world there is separation, harsher than any pain; and in the Hereafter, the burning, more terrible than any trial. Preferring the little of the fleeting deprives one of the much of the eternal.

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ح ي ي / د ن و
mots.sens: they prefer the life of this world

mots.racine: ص د د / س ب ل
mots.sens: they turn from the path of God

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ع و ج
ض ل ل / ب ع د

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا بِلِسَانِ قَوْمِهِ لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمْ ۖ فَيُضِلُّ اللَّهُ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشَاءُ ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnā min rasūlin illā bi-lisāni qawmihi li-yubayyina lahum fa-yuḍillu llāhu man yashāʾu wa-yahdī man yashāʾu wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: We have sent no messenger except in the tongue of his people, that he might make things clear to them. God leads astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills; and He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is so in order to make the proof more binding still — yet what use is the clarity of proof if one is not granted success to walk the way? The people of guidance won by the prior care; the people of straying fell into the humiliation of estrangement. None may object to what He does, nor is He questioned about anything — He acts at the root, where no objection has any standing.

mots.racine: ل س ن / ق و م
ب ي ن

mots.ar: فَيُضِلُّ اللَّهُ مَنْ يَشَاءُ وَيَهْدِي مَنْ يَشَاءُ
mots.tr: fa-yuḍillu llāhu man yashāʾu wa-yahdī man yashāʾu
mots.racine: ض ل ل / ه د ي / ش ي أ
mots.sens: God strays whom He wills and guides whom He wills
mots.def: yuḍillu / yahdī: verbs of divine acting.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: He is the Mighty, the Wise

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِآيَاتِنَا أَنْ أَخْرِجْ قَوْمَكَ مِنَ الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَذَكِّرْهُمْ بِأَيَّامِ اللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ
versets.tr: wa-laqad arsalnā mūsā bi-āyātinā an akhrij qawmaka mina ẓ-ẓulumāti ilā n-nūri wa-dhakkirhum bi-ayyāmi llāhi inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-kulli ṣabbārin shakūrin
versets.trad: Thus We sent Moses with Our signs [saying]: “Bring forth your people from the darknesses into the light, and remind them of the Days of God.” Indeed, in that are signs for every patient, grateful one.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: bring forth your people, by your call, from the darknesses of their doubt into the light of certainty, and from the obscurities of ignorance into the freshness of knowledge. Remind them of the Days of God: what was given them at the time of the Covenant, and the trials lifted from them in their former states. And he adds, on a higher reading: the Days of God are those when the servant's soul was still in the veil of non-being, while the Real was already taking care of His servants before any deed of theirs. The “patient” holds firm to His decree; the “grateful” lets himself be traversed by His gift.

mots.racine: أ ي ي
خ ر ج / ق و م
mots.sens: that you bring forth your people

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ي و م
mots.sens: remind them of the Days of God
mots.def: ayyām Allāh: events of His manifestation.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ش ك ر
mots.def: ṣabbār: very patient; shakūr: very grateful.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ أَنْجَاكُمْ مِنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوءَ الْعَذَابِ وَيُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْنَاءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَاءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِي ذَٰلِكُمْ بَلَاءٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla mūsā li-qawmihi dhkurū niʿmata llāhi ʿalaykum idh anjākum min āli firʿawna yasūmūnakum sūʾa l-ʿadhābi wa-yudhabbiḥūna abnāʾakum wa-yastaḥyūna nisāʾakum wa-fī dhālikum balāʾun min rabbikum ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: When Moses said to his people: “Remember God's favor upon you when He delivered you from the people of Pharaoh, who inflicted on you the worst torment, slaughtering your sons and sparing your daughters. In that was a tremendous trial from your Lord.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the remembrance of past favors revives past love, for hearts are kneaded with love for whoever has done them good. The Real commanded Moses to remind his people of the kinds of His gifts and the delicacies of His honoring; and in certain Books revealed to the prophets He says: “O My servant, I love you — so by My right upon you, be for Me a lover.” Reminding is the work of love's memory.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ن ع م
ن ج و
mots.sens: when He saved you from Pharaoh's people

mots.racine: س و م / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: they inflicted on you the worst torment

mots.racine: ب ل و / ر ب ب / ع ظ م
mots.sens: an immense trial from your Lord

versets.ar: وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكُمْ لَئِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ ۖ وَلَئِنْ كَفَرْتُمْ إِنَّ عَذَابِي لَشَدِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh ta-adhdhana rabbukum la-in shakartum la-azīdannakum wa-la-in kafartum inna ʿadhābī la-shadīdun
versets.trad: And remember when your Lord proclaimed: “If you are grateful, I shall surely increase you; but if you are ungrateful, indeed My punishment is severe!”
versets.commentaire: On this word Qushayrī unfolds a cascade: “If you are grateful, I shall increase My favors for you; if you deny My blessing, I shall punish you today with trial, and tomorrow with separation and estrangement. If you recognize My union, I shall increase your share of My gift to the contemplation of My beauty and My majesty. If you thank the paths of success in worship, I shall increase by the realization of will. If you thank the vision of recompense, I shall increase by the vision of My attributes. If you thank the kinds of My gifts, I shall increase by the vision of My honor, then by the vision of My power. If you thank what We have entrusted you of My gifts, I shall increase by what We have promised you of My encounter.” The ladder of gratitude climbs from gift to encounter.

mots.racine: أ ذ ن
ش ك ر / ز ي د
mots.sens: if you are grateful I shall increase you

mots.racine: ك ف ر
ع ذ ب / ش د د

versets.ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ إِنْ تَكْفُرُوا أَنْتُمْ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَغَنِيٌّ حَمِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla mūsā in takfurū antum wa-man fī l-arḍi jamīʿan fa-inna llāha la-ghaniyyun ḥamīdun
versets.trad: And Moses said: “Even if you should disbelieve — you and all those on earth — God is truly Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paraphrases: were you all to gather, you and those who back you, and all who follow your tracks, to deny Him together — and were you each day to take a host of associates — you would not inflict any blemish on His glory; just as, were you to thank Him, you would add no ornament to His sovereignty. By His attributes and the grandeur of His omnipotence, He is exalted; toward the entire world, He is rich.

mots.ar: إِنْ تَكْفُرُوا أَنْتُمْ وَمَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا
mots.tr: in takfurū antum wa-man fī l-arḍi jamīʿan
mots.racine: ك ف ر / أ ر ض
mots.sens: if you deny, you and all on earth

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح م د

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَبَأُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعَادٍ وَثَمُودَ ۛ وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ ۛ لَا يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا اللَّهُ ۚ جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَرَدُّوا أَيْدِيَهُمْ فِي أَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَقَالُوا إِنَّا كَفَرْنَا بِمَا أُرْسِلْتُمْ بِهِ وَإِنَّا لَفِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا تَدْعُونَنَا إِلَيْهِ مُرِيبٍ
versets.tr: a-lam yaʾtikum nabaʾu lladhīna min qablikum qawmi nūḥin wa-ʿādin wa-thamūda wa-lladhīna min baʿdihim lā yaʿlamuhum illā llāhu jāʾathum rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti fa-raddū aydiyahum fī afwāhihim wa-qālū innā kafarnā bi-mā ursiltum bihi wa-innā la-fī shakkin mim-mā tadʿūnanā ilayhi murībin
versets.trad: Has there not come to you the tidings of those before you — the people of Noah, ʿĀd, Thamūd, and those after them whom only God knows? Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they put their hands to their mouths and said: “We disbelieve in that with which you have been sent, and we are in grave doubt about what you call us to.”
versets.commentaire: A question posed for conviction. Qushayrī: when the messengers came to them, they received them with ingratitude and treated them with denial; they put their hands in their mouths in a sign of contempt, followed the way of their like in disbelief, and built their principles on doubt and suspicion; they founded their doctrines on association and straying. The gesture of hand to mouth speaks the scornful laugh and the closure to every word.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / ق ب ل
mots.sens: the tidings of those before you

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ر س ل / ب ي ن
mots.sens: their messengers came with the proofs

mots.racine: ر د د / ي د ي / ف و ه
mots.sens: they put their hands to their mouths
mots.def: gesture of contempt or biting fingers.

mots.ar: وَإِنَّا لَفِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا تَدْعُونَنَا إِلَيْهِ مُرِيبٍ
mots.tr: wa-innā la-fī shakkin mim-mā tadʿūnanā ilayhi murīb
mots.racine: ش ك ك / د ع و / ر ي ب

versets.ar: قَالَتْ رُسُلُهُمْ أَفِي اللَّهِ شَكٌّ فَاطِرِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِيَغْفِرَ لَكُمْ مِنْ ذُنُوبِكُمْ وَيُؤَخِّرَكُمْ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ۚ قَالُوا إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنَا تُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَصُدُّونَا عَمَّا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ آبَاؤُنَا فَأْتُونَا بِسُلْطَانٍ مُبِينٍ
versets.tr: qālat rusuluhum a-fī llāhi shakkun fāṭiri s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi yadʿūkum li-yaghfira lakum min dhunūbikum wa-yuʾakhkhirakum ilā ajalin musamman qālū in antum illā basharun mithlunā turīdūna an taṣuddūnā ʿammā kāna yaʿbudu ābāʾunā fa-ʾtūnā bi-sulṭānin mubīnin
versets.trad: Their messengers said: “Can there be doubt about God, Originator of the heavens and the earth? He calls you to forgive you your sins and to give you respite until an appointed term.” They replied: “You are only mortals like ourselves. You want to turn us from what our fathers used to worship. So bring us a clear authority!”
versets.commentaire: A question that amounts to negation: no breath stirs without His disposing. How can the majesty of His power be seen except by one whose eye His grace has anointed with light? And on “He calls you to forgive you”: Qushayrī: what is astonishing is not the servant who bears for his Master labors and what is unbearable, never fleeing service nor seeking ease. The astonishing thing is this mighty and generous Lord who calls His servant to forgive him, though he has failed, and treats him with benevolence, though he has shirked.

mots.racine: ش ك ك
ف ط ر
mots.sens: Originator of the heavens and earth
mots.def: fāṭir: who brings forth from nothing.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ذ ن ب
ب ش ر / م ث ل
mots.sens: you are but mortals like us

mots.racine: س ل ط / ب ي ن

versets.ar: قَالَتْ لَهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ إِنْ نَحْنُ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُكُمْ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَمُنُّ عَلَىٰ مَنْ يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لَنَا أَنْ نَأْتِيَكُمْ بِسُلْطَانٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۚ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: qālat lahum rusuluhum in naḥnu illā basharun mithlukum wa-lākinna llāha yamunnu ʿalā man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi wa-mā kāna lanā an naʾtiyakum bi-sulṭānin illā bi-idhni llāhi wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-muʾminūna
versets.trad: Their messengers replied: “We are but mortals like you, yet God bestows His favor on whom He wills among His servants. It is not for us to bring you an authority except by God's permission. And upon God let the believers rely!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī has the messengers say: we are only your likes; the difference between us is that He has gratified us, glory to Him, by His knowledge, and singled us out by what He has set us apart with in honoring. As for the signs you demand of us, we have no way to bring them, unless He makes them appear through us, when He wills and how He wills — He is capable of that. Prophecy is a gift, not a feat.

mots.racine: م ن ن / ش ي أ
أ ذ ن
و ك ل / أ م ن
mots.sens: let the believers trust in God

versets.ar: وَمَا لَنَا أَلَّا نَتَوَكَّلَ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَقَدْ هَدَانَا سُبُلَنَا ۚ وَلَنَصْبِرَنَّ عَلَىٰ مَا آذَيْتُمُونَا ۚ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā lanā allā natawakkala ʿalā llāhi wa-qad hadānā subulanā wa-la-naṣbiranna ʿalā mā ādhaytumūnā wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-mutawakkilūna
versets.trad: Why should we not rely upon God when He has guided us in our ways? We will surely bear with patience the harm you do to us. And upon God let those who trust place their trust!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “Why should we not rely on God, when He has raised us from the degree of obligation by proof to the finding of the spirit of evidence, through all He has poured upon us of beautiful benevolence? That suffices us as dignity.” And on patience: patience in trial is lightened when it stands under the vision of the One who tries. To see the Giver makes the gift bearable, even when bitter.

mots.ar: وَمَا لَنَا أَلَّا نَتَوَكَّلَ عَلَى اللَّهِ
mots.tr: wa-mā lanā allā natawakkala ʿalā llāh
mots.racine: و ك ل
mots.sens: what have we that we should not trust God

mots.racine: ه د ي / س ب ل
mots.sens: when He has guided us in our ways

mots.racine: ص ب ر / أ ذ ي
mots.sens: we shall surely bear your harm
mots.def: ṣabara: to be patient; ādhā: to harm.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِرُسُلِهِمْ لَنُخْرِجَنَّكُمْ مِنْ أَرْضِنَا أَوْ لَتَعُودُنَّ فِي مِلَّتِنَا ۖ فَأَوْحَىٰ إِلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ لَنُهْلِكَنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū li-rusulihim la-nukhrijannakum min arḍinā aw la-taʿūdunna fī millatinā fa-awḥā ilayhim rabbuhum la-nuhlikanna ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: The disbelievers said to their messengers: “We will surely drive you out of our land, unless you return to our religion.” Then their Lord revealed to them: “We will surely destroy the wrongdoers,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the enemies found themselves powerless to oppose the prophets with the like of their signs, they turned to mistreating them with all sorts of warnings and threats — expulsion from homelands, scattering through the lands. But God spread upon their hearts, by the promise of His help and His meeting, what shaded them in the trial, and showed them in advance the dwellings of their enemies, strengthening their hearts to endure. Where man threatens, the Real consoles.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / أ ر ض
mots.sens: we shall expel you from our land

mots.racine: ع و د / م ل ل
mots.sens: or you return to our religion

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ظ ل م
mots.sens: We shall surely destroy the wrongdoers

versets.ar: وَلَنُسْكِنَنَّكُمُ الْأَرْضَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَنْ خَافَ مَقَامِي وَخَافَ وَعِيدِ
versets.tr: wa-la-nuskinannakumu l-arḍa min baʿdihim dhālika li-man khāfa maqāmī wa-khāfa waʿīdi
versets.trad: and We will surely settle you in the land after them. This is for whoever fears My Station and fears My threat.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two fears: “whoever fears My Station (maqām)” means he has feared his own station instead of tomorrow's reckoning, and returned to himself by way of election. And it is said: “whoever feared My Station” means he dreaded My gaze upon him. The first is the reminder of the future reckoning; the second, the realization of present vigilance (murāqaba). Both fears open the dwelling.

mots.racine: س ك ن / أ ر ض
mots.sens: We shall settle you in the land

mots.racine: خ و ف / ق و م
mots.def: maqām: the station (of judgment, of vigilance).

mots.racine: و ع د

versets.ar: وَاسْتَفْتَحُوا وَخَابَ كُلُّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-staftaḥū wa-khāba kullu jabbārin ʿanīdin
versets.trad: The messengers sought victory, and every obstinate tyrant came to ruin.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls that istiftāḥ means to seek the opening, and the opening is the decree. Some demanded that the punishment fall upon them, out of defiance — as in: “if this be the Truth from You, rain down upon us stones from the sky.” When the trial fell, neither their supplications nor their tears availed; no alms or ransom was accepted; they repented when no repentance remains. And he adds: when the trial intensifies and the invocation becomes sincere, deliverance is near.

mots.racine: ف ت ح
mots.def: istaftaḥa: to seek victory or judgment.

mots.racine: خ ي ب / ج ب ر / ع ن د

versets.ar: مِنْ وَرَائِهِ جَهَنَّمُ وَيُسْقَىٰ مِنْ مَاءٍ صَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: min warāʾihi jahannamu wa-yusqā min māʾin ṣadīdin
versets.trad: Before him is Hell, and he is made to drink fetid water
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes that the word warāʾ can designate what is in front as well as what is behind — it speaks of what is veiled (tawārā) upon you. Thus the disbeliever: punishment reaches him in what is before him in time, and in what is behind him — that is, for what has passed of his foul deeds. He is made to drink of the fire one gulp after another, for its harshness and bitterness forbid him to swallow it in one draught.

mots.racine: و ر ي / ج ه ن م
س ق ي / ص د د
mots.sens: he is given to drink fetid water

versets.ar: يَتَجَرَّعُهُ وَلَا يَكَادُ يُسِيغُهُ وَيَأْتِيهِ الْمَوْتُ مِنْ كُلِّ مَكَانٍ وَمَا هُوَ بِمَيِّتٍ ۖ وَمِنْ وَرَائِهِ عَذَابٌ غَلِيظٌ
versets.tr: yatajarraʿuhu wa-lā yakādu yusīghuhu wa-yaʾtīhi l-mawtu min kulli makānin wa-mā huwa bi-mayyitin wa-min warāʾihi ʿadhābun ghalīẓun
versets.trad: which he gulps but can hardly swallow. Death assails him from every side, yet he cannot die. Beyond him stands a relentless punishment.
themes: the death that does not finish
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he sees the punishment — so intense it is — in every limb, at every moment, in every place. And it is not death, for the people of the Fire do not die; but it is, in intensity, like death. “And beyond him is a dense punishment”: it is eternity in the Fire — such is the recompense of one who let himself be seduced by a few brief days in which the Decree was kind to him, and who was deceived without sensing what would follow.

mots.racine: ج ر ع / س و غ
mots.sens: he gulps it down with great difficulty
mots.def: tajarraʿa: to swallow in gulps; asāgha: to make pass.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / م و ت / ك و ن
mots.sens: death comes to him from everywhere

mots.racine: م و ت
ع ذ ب / غ ل ظ

versets.ar: مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ ۖ أَعْمَالُهُمْ كَرَمَادٍ اشْتَدَّتْ بِهِ الرِّيحُ فِي يَوْمٍ عَاصِفٍ ۖ لَا يَقْدِرُونَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا عَلَىٰ شَيْءٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الضَّلَالُ الْبَعِيدُ
versets.tr: mathalu lladhīna kafarū bi-rabbihim aʿmāluhum ka-ramādin ishtaddat bihi r-rīḥu fī yawmin ʿāṣifin lā yaqdirūna mim-mā kasabū ʿalā shayʾin dhālika huwa ḍ-ḍalālu l-baʿīdu
versets.trad: The likeness of those who disbelieve in their Lord: their deeds are as ashes upon which the wind rages on a stormy day. They have no power over anything they have earned. That is the profound straying.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: among what is recited to you, O Muḥammad, there is a parable of the deeds of the disbelievers in their vanishing, and of how nothing of them is accepted — like ashes on a stormy day: nothing remains. Such are their deeds. And whoever is so has lost in both abodes, and woe (wayl) has settled upon him. Action without faith is light ash; one breath suffices to scatter it.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ر م د

mots.ar: اشْتَدَّتْ بِهِ الرِّيحُ فِي يَوْمٍ عَاصِفٍ
mots.tr: ishtaddat bihi r-rīḥu fī yawmin ʿāṣif
mots.racine: ش د د / ر و ح / ع ص ف
mots.sens: the wind rages on a stormy day

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ع د

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ ۚ إِنْ يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: a-lam tara anna llāha khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bi-l-ḥaqqi in yashaʾ yudhhibkum wa-yaʾti bi-khalqin jadīdin
versets.trad: Do you not see that God created the heavens and the earth in truth? If He willed, He could take you away and bring forth a new creation.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He created the heavens and the earth by the decree of the True — that is, He has the right to that by the truth of His sovereignty — and He created them by His true word. He made every part of them an indication of His oneness, and a way for whoever wills to reach his Lord. Then: if He wills, He removes you by extinguishing and brings forth a new creation by instauration.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ح ق ق
mots.sens: He created heavens and earth in truth

mots.racine: ش ي أ / ذ ه ب
mots.sens: if He wills He takes you away

mots.racine: أ ت ي / خ ل ق / ج د د

versets.ar: وَمَا ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ بِعَزِيزٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā dhālika ʿalā llāhi bi-ʿazīzin
versets.trad: That is no difficult thing for God.
versets.commentaire: A brief verse suspended from the preceding. Qushayrī: that is not difficult for Him — and how could it be, when He has power over all things? The “to annihilate and bring forth anew” does not weigh in His hand, for His power knows neither effort nor measure.

mots.racine: ع ز ز
mots.sens: that is not difficult for God
mots.def: ʿazīz: here, arduous, out of reach.

versets.ar: وَبَرَزُوا لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا فَقَالَ الضُّعَفَاءُ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعًا فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُغْنُونَ عَنَّا مِنْ عَذَابِ اللَّهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ۚ قَالُوا لَوْ هَدَانَا اللَّهُ لَهَدَيْنَاكُمْ ۖ سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْنَا أَجَزِعْنَا أَمْ صَبَرْنَا مَا لَنَا مِنْ مَحِيصٍ
versets.tr: wa-barazū lillāhi jamīʿan fa-qāla ḍ-ḍuʿafāʾu lilladhīna stakbarū innā kunnā lakum tabaʿan fa-hal antum mughnūna ʿannā min ʿadhābi llāhi min shayʾin qālū law hadānā llāhu la-hadaynākum sawāʾun ʿalaynā a-jaziʿnā am ṣabarnā mā lanā min maḥīṣin
versets.trad: All shall appear before God. The weak will say to those who were proud: “We followed you; can you now spare us anything of God's punishment?” They will reply: “Had God guided us, we would have guided you. It is all one to us whether we grieve or endure: there is no escape for us.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they were not hidden from the Real, that they should now appear to Him — their appearing means that their knowings have become necessary and that they have arrived at stations where none but God has power: as though they appeared to Him. The weak said to the arrogant: “We were your followers” — hoping they would remove from them some hardship. But the arrogant replied: “We are all together in the punishment; if we could lift anything from you, or show you a way of salvation, we would have done so.” Imitation without discernment leads to a common dead end.

mots.racine: ب ر ز
ض ع ف / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the weak shall say to the arrogant

mots.racine: غ ن ي
ح ي ص

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ لَمَّا قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَعَدَكُمْ وَعْدَ الْحَقِّ وَوَعَدْتُكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُكُمْ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لِيَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ سُلْطَانٍ إِلَّا أَنْ دَعَوْتُكُمْ فَاسْتَجَبْتُمْ لِي ۖ فَلَا تَلُومُونِي وَلُومُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ ۖ مَا أَنَا بِمُصْرِخِكُمْ وَمَا أَنْتُمْ بِمُصْرِخِيَّ ۖ إِنِّي كَفَرْتُ بِمَا أَشْرَكْتُمُونِ مِنْ قَبْلُ ۗ إِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla sh-shayṭānu lammā quḍiya l-amru inna llāha waʿadakum waʿda l-ḥaqqi wa-waʿadtukum fa-akhlaftukum wa-mā kāna liya ʿalaykum min sulṭānin illā an daʿawtukum fa-stajabtum lī fa-lā talūmūnī wa-lūmū anfusakum mā anā bi-muṣrikhikum wa-mā antum bi-muṣrikhiyya innī kafartu bi-mā ashraktumūni min qablu inna ẓ-ẓālimīna lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: When the matter is decided, Satan will say: “Indeed God promised you a true promise, and I promised you, then failed you. I had no authority over you except that I called you, and you answered me. So blame me not, but blame yourselves. I cannot help you, nor can you help me. I deny what you formerly associated with me.” For the wrongdoers is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī has Satan say, in paraphrase: “Do not blame me, blame yourselves — and the time of blame is over. Self-blame profits only in the time of respite and the moments of obligation, when the doors of repentance are open; but for one whose spirit has departed, they are shut.” Satan pleads that he only called: the answer was the human's. Straying is never a constraint; it is a consent.

mots.racine: ق ض ي / أ م ر
و ع د / ح ق ق
mots.sens: He made you a true promise

mots.racine: و ع د / خ ل ف
mots.sens: I promised you and broke my word

mots.racine: ل و م / ن ف س
mots.sens: do not blame me, blame yourselves

mots.ar: إِنِّي كَفَرْتُ بِمَا أَشْرَكْتُمُونِ مِنْ قَبْلُ
mots.tr: innī kafartu bi-mā ashraktumūni min qablu
mots.racine: ك ف ر / ش ر ك
mots.sens: I deny the association you made of me

versets.ar: وَأُدْخِلَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ تَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيهَا سَلَامٌ
versets.tr: wa-udkhila lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā bi-idhni rabbihim taḥiyyatuhum fīhā salāmun
versets.trad: But those who believed and did righteous deeds shall be admitted into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever by their Lord's permission. Their greeting therein will be: “Peace (salām)!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: faith is assent; “and they performed the righteous deeds” is the realization of that assent. Into the righteous deeds enter all sorts of goods, however small — even removing some filthy thing from the path. And on the greeting: their states in the degrees differ: one group is delivered from burning, then from separation, then from punishment, then from the veil. The degrees of Paradise culminate where the veil (ḥijāb) itself lifts.

mots.racine: د خ ل / أ م ن / ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.sens: those who believed and did good shall be admitted

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ج ر ي / ن ه ر
ح ي ي / س ل م

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلًا كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً كَشَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ أَصْلُهَا ثَابِتٌ وَفَرْعُهَا فِي السَّمَاءِ
versets.tr: a-lam tara kayfa ḍaraba llāhu mathalan kalimatan ṭayyibatan ka-shajaratin ṭayyibatin aṣluhā thābitun wa-farʿuhā fī s-samāʾi
versets.trad: Have you not seen how God strikes a parable? A good word is like a good tree whose root is firm and whose branches reach the sky. It yields its fruit at every season
themes: the tree of the good word
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this is a parable of faith and of the knowledge of Him, glory to Him. He compares it to a good tree whose root is fixed in the earth and whose branches are high, whose fruits are abundant — it gives its nourishment at every moment. The root of this tree is knowledge; the trunk, faith strengthened by proofs; the branches, the good deeds which are the obligations and the avoidance of disobediences. The leaves are the holding to the courtesies of servitude; the flowers, the beautiful virtues; the fruits, the sweetness of obedience and the delight of service. And it is also said: the good word is the testimony of oneness (shahāda); it is good only when it issues from a sincere inner secret.

mots.racine: ك ل م / ط ي ب
mots.def: kalima ṭayyiba: pure word, the tawḥīd.

mots.racine: ش ج ر
أ ص ل / ث ب ت
ف ر ع / س م و

versets.ar: تُؤْتِي أُكُلَهَا كُلَّ حِينٍ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهَا ۗ وَيَضْرِبُ اللَّهُ الْأَمْثَالَ لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: tuʾtī ukulahā kulla ḥīnin bi-idhni rabbihā wa-yaḍribu llāhu l-amthāla lin-nāsi laʿallahum yatadhakkarūna
versets.trad: by its Lord's permission. Thus God strikes parables for mankind, that they may remember.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues the image: the fruits of trees ripen only once a year; the fruits of this tree come at every moment, and many times more. As God has said of the reward of Paradise: “neither cut off nor forbidden” — so are the delicacies of this tree: neither cut off nor forbidden; the hearts of the people of realities are neither turned from them nor veiled; it shows itself to them at every breath, unveiled.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ ك ل / ح ي ن
mots.sens: it gives its fruit every season

mots.racine: أ ذ ن / ر ب ب
ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.def: ḍaraba mathalan: to strike a parable.

versets.ar: وَمَثَلُ كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ كَشَجَرَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ اجْتُثَّتْ مِنْ فَوْقِ الْأَرْضِ مَا لَهَا مِنْ قَرَارٍ
versets.tr: wa-mathalu kalimatin khabīthatin ka-shajaratin khabīthatin ijtuththat min fawqi l-arḍi mā lahā min qarārin
versets.trad: And a bad word is like a bad tree: uprooted from the surface of the earth, having no stability.
themes: the tree of the bad word
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the parable of a bad word is that of the word of disbelief; its badness comes from the impurity of association that accompanies it — the word is bad because it issues from a heart that is the seat and source of association. The bad tree is association: uprooted from the soil, because disbelief contradicts and opposes itself; it has neither sound root, nor binding proof, nor disclosing indication, nor compelling cause. They are only illusions, vanities, and straying — without any foundation (qarār), born of broken bases.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ك ل م / خ ب ث
mots.sens: the parable of a bad word

mots.racine: ج ث ث / أ ر ض
mots.sens: uprooted from the surface of the earth

mots.racine: ق ر ر

versets.ar: يُثَبِّتُ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِالْقَوْلِ الثَّابِتِ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ ۖ وَيُضِلُّ اللَّهُ الظَّالِمِينَ ۚ وَيَفْعَلُ اللَّهُ مَا يَشَاءُ
versets.tr: yuthabbitu llāhu lladhīna āmanū bi-l-qawli th-thābiti fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-fī l-ākhirati wa-yuḍillu llāhu ẓ-ẓālimīna wa-yafʿalu llāhu mā yashāʾu
versets.trad: God strengthens those who believe with the firm Word in this life and the Hereafter, and He lets the wrongdoers stray. God does what He wills.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds several readings of the firm Word: it is the holding to rectitude and the abandoning of crookedness; it is the necessary shahāda issuing from the purity of dogma and the limpidness of the secret; it is the word uttered by the hearts, not by the tongue; it is the Word of God, the Mighty, the Eternal, to whom extinction is not fitting — more worthy of constancy than the servant's word, for the servant's word is only an effect. In this world He strengthens it, that no innovation touch it; in the Hereafter, by His messengers among the angels; on the Standing Day, at the questioning and reckoning; in Paradise, for the servant's praise of God and his knowledge of Him do not vanish.

mots.racine: ث ب ت / أ م ن
ق و ل / ث ب ت
ض ل ل / ظ ل م
ف ع ل / ش ي أ

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ بَدَّلُوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ كُفْرًا وَأَحَلُّوا قَوْمَهُمْ دَارَ الْبَوَارِ
versets.tr: a-lam tara ilā lladhīna baddalū niʿmata llāhi kufran wa-aḥallū qawmahum dāra l-bawāri
versets.trad: Have you not seen those who exchange God's favor for disbelief, and bring their people down to the abode of ruin —
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī expands the betrayal of the favor to the whole spiritual life: they put ingratitude in the place of gratitude; they used the favor for disbelief, instead of using it for what it called for — recognition. Using the favor in disobedience is part of this: the servant's limbs are all favors of God; when the disobedient uses his body for the slip rather than obedience, he has changed the favor into disbelief. Likewise whoever houses heedlessness in his heart in place of knowledge, attachment instead of rupture toward Him, who hangs his heart on others instead of trust in Him, who soils his tongue with the mention of creatures and their praise instead of the mention of God — all this is changing God's favors into disbelief.

mots.racine: ب د ل / ن ع م / ك ف ر
mots.sens: they changed God's favor for disbelief

mots.racine: ح ل ل / د و ر / ب و ر
mots.sens: they lodged their people in the abode of ruin

versets.ar: جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَوْنَهَا ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْقَرَارُ
versets.tr: jahannama yaṣlawnahā wa-biʾsa l-qarāru
versets.trad: Hell, where they shall burn? What an evil resting-place!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums it in one stroke: it is the brought-forward Gehenna — and its punishment is separation, not burning. For the mystic, the worst of Hell is not the flame: it is the estrangement (firqa). The flesh burns; the heart moans for being far.

mots.racine: ج ه ن م / ص ل ي
ب ء س / ق ر ر

versets.ar: وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ أَنْدَادًا لِيُضِلُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ ۗ قُلْ تَمَتَّعُوا فَإِنَّ مَصِيرَكُمْ إِلَى النَّارِ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalū lillāhi andādan li-yuḍillū ʿan sabīlihi qul tamattaʿū fa-inna maṣīrakum ilā n-nāri
versets.trad: They set up rivals to God to lead others astray from His path. Say: “Enjoy! Your journey ends in the Fire.”
themes: enjoy and end in the Fire
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they consented that their handiwork should be their adored, and their carving their goal. They turned aside from the way of rectitude, and went far from the dwelling of honor. Tomorrow they will find the consequences of their handiwork, as it was said: “Enjoy a few brief days, for the days of joy are short, and the delights of heedlessness extinguish quickly.” To make the work of one's hands one's god is the most radical inversion.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ن د د
ق و ل / م ت ع
ص ي ر / ن و ر
mots.sens: your destiny is to the Fire

versets.ar: قُلْ لِعِبَادِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَيُنْفِقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْنَاهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لَا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلَا خِلَالٌ
versets.tr: qul li-ʿibādiya lladhīna āmanū yuqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-yunfiqū mim-mā razaqnāhum sirran wa-ʿalāniyatan min qabli an yaʾtiya yawmun lā bayʿun fīhi wa-lā khilālun
versets.trad: Tell My believing servants to perform the prayer and to spend from what We have provided them, secretly and openly, before there comes a Day on which there shall be neither bargain nor friendship.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God has placed all the servant's rest today in the prayer — for it is the place of intimate confiding (munājāt). The Messenger said: “Give us rest by the prayer, O Bilāl.” The prayer is the opening of the door of provision. And in the prayer the servant pours out his secrets with the Real. And on spending: He commanded them to spend of the tongue for His remembrance, of the body for His obedience, of time for His thanksgiving, of the heart for His knowledge, of the spirit for His love, and of the inmost secret for His contemplation.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
ن ف ق / ر ز ق
mots.sens: and spend of what We provided them

mots.racine: س ر ر / ع ل ن
ب ي ع / خ ل ل

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَأَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَخْرَجَ بِهِ مِنَ الثَّمَرَاتِ رِزْقًا لَكُمْ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الْفُلْكَ لِتَجْرِيَ فِي الْبَحْرِ بِأَمْرِهِ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الْأَنْهَارَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-akhraja bihi mina th-thamarāti rizqan lakum wa-sakhkhara lakumu l-fulka li-tajriya fī l-baḥri bi-amrihi wa-sakhkhara lakumu l-anhāra
versets.trad: God is He who created the heavens and the earth and sent down water from the sky, by which He brought forth fruits for your provision. He has placed the ship at your service that it might sail upon the sea by His command, and He has placed the rivers at your service.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens a double reading: on the outer plane, He raised the heaven and made it high, spread the earth beneath, placed in it seas, made rivers flow, made trees grow, set lights and flowers, rained an abundant water, brought forth fruits of distinct varieties and savors, set for each a ripe time. But on the inner plane — the sky of hearts: He adorned it with the lamps of intellects, raised in it the sun of unification (tawḥīd) and the moon of knowledge (ʿirfān). He made the two seas of fear (khawf) and hope (rajāʾ) converge in the hearts, and placed between them a barrier that they may not overflow. The ship, then, is the ship of assistance and preservation, the vessel of lights and guarding.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
mots.sens: He created the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م و ه
mots.sens: He sent down from the sky water

mots.racine: س خ ر / ف ل ك
mots.sens: He placed the ship at your service

mots.racine: س خ ر / ن ه ر
mots.sens: He placed the rivers at your service

versets.ar: وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ دَائِبَيْنِ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهَارَ
versets.tr: wa-sakhkhara lakumu sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara dāʾibayni wa-sakhkhara lakumu l-layla wa-n-nahāra
versets.trad: He has placed the sun and the moon at your service, in their constant courses, and He has placed the night and the day at your service.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī applies night and day to spiritual states: the nights of seeking for the aspirants; the nights of rapture for the people of intimacy among the lovers; the nights of struggle for the repentant. And likewise the day of the knowers, in their independence from the lamp of knowledge, when the day of certainty rises. Sun and moon constant in the sky become a metaphor for the regularity of divine gifts in the sky of the heart.

mots.racine: ش م س / ق م ر / د أ ب
mots.sens: the sun and the moon assiduous

mots.racine: ل ي ل / ن ه ر

versets.ar: وَآتَاكُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ مَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُ ۚ وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا ۗ إِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ لَظَلُومٌ كَفَّارٌ
versets.tr: wa-ātākum min kulli mā saʾaltumūhu wa-in taʿuddū niʿmata llāhi lā tuḥṣūhā inna l-insāna la-ẓalūmun kaffārun
versets.trad: He has given you of all you have asked Him. If you tried to count God's favors, you could not number them. Indeed man is most wrongdoing and most ungrateful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, on the innumerable favor: what your high aspirations have aimed at, what your asking has touched, what the realization has imagined in your thoughts — We have granted you beyond your hopes, and given you more than you count on. How could your gratitude match His favors? Your thanks is little and weak; His grace is ample and abundant. How could the drop of gratitude hold against the oceans of giving? And he adds, dizzyingly: the very capacity to thank is yet another gift; when the servant wants to thank, he can only do so by another gift — so that no favor remains without calling forth a new thanks.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / س أ ل
mots.sens: He has given you of all you have asked

mots.ar: وَإِنْ تَعُدُّوا نِعْمَتَ اللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَا
mots.tr: wa-in taʿuddū niʿmata llāhi lā tuḥṣūhā
mots.racine: ع د د / ن ع م / ح ص و
mots.sens: if you would count the favor you could not number it

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ك ف ر
mots.sens: man is most wrongdoing and most ungrateful

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هَٰذَا الْبَلَدَ آمِنًا وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَنْ نَعْبُدَ الْأَصْنَامَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qāla ibrāhīmu rabbi-jʿal hādhā l-balada āminan wa-jnubnī wa-baniyya an naʿbuda l-aṣnāma
versets.trad: When Abraham said: “My Lord! Make this land secure, and keep me and my sons from worshipping idols, for,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī opens the invocation beyond Mecca: as he asked that Mecca be a land of safety, he asked that his heart be a dwelling of safety — that is, that there be in it nothing but by God. “Keep me, and my sons, from worshipping idols”: and the idol is whatever is worshipped besides Him. As God has said: “Have you seen the one who has taken his passion as his god?” Thus the idol of each is what occupies him and turns him from God — wealth, child, dignity, obedience, worship themselves. To ask that the land be safe is to ask that the heart be pure of every co-adored.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ب ل د / أ م ن
ج ن ب / ب ن ي
mots.sens: keep me and my sons away
mots.def: jannaba: to keep at a distance.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ص ن م

versets.ar: رَبِّ إِنَّهُنَّ أَضْلَلْنَ كَثِيرًا مِنَ النَّاسِ ۖ فَمَنْ تَبِعَنِي فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي ۖ وَمَنْ عَصَانِي فَإِنَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ
versets.tr: rabbi innahunna aḍlalna kathīran mina n-nāsi fa-man tabiʿanī fa-innahu minnī wa-man ʿaṣānī fa-innaka ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: my Lord! They have led many astray. Whoever follows me is of me; but whoever disobeys me — You are Forgiving, Compassionate.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “Whoever follows me is of me” — that is, conformable to me, of the people of my community; and whoever has disobeyed me has opposed me and disobeyed You. “You are Forgiving, Compassionate”: a request for mercy by way of allusion — that is, “have mercy upon them.” He said “whoever has disobeyed me” and not “whoever has disobeyed You,” though disobedience to him is disobedience to God; but the expression aims to request mercy for what touches his own right, which he releases, and which he meets with mercy rather than claim.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ك ث ر
ت ب ع
mots.sens: whoever follows me is of me

mots.racine: ع ص ي / غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.sens: whoever disobeys me, You are Forgiving, Compassionate

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا إِنِّي أَسْكَنْتُ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي بِوَادٍ غَيْرِ ذِي زَرْعٍ عِنْدَ بَيْتِكَ الْمُحَرَّمِ رَبَّنَا لِيُقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ وَارْزُقْهُمْ مِنَ الثَّمَرَاتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: rabbanā innī askantu min dhurriyyatī bi-wādin ghayri dhī zarʿin ʿinda baytika l-muḥarrami rabbanā li-yuqīmū ṣ-ṣalāta fa-jʿal afʾidatan mina n-nāsi tahwī ilayhim wa-rzuqhum mina th-thamarāti laʿallahum yashkurūna
versets.trad: Our Lord! I have settled part of my offspring in a barren valley, near Your sacred House — our Lord! — that they may perform the prayer. Make the hearts of some people incline toward them, and provide them with fruits, that they may be grateful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he spoke the truth of his confident reliance and the sincerity of his abandon: “I have settled my offspring”… And he saw the care for them in the neighborhood, not in the provisions — “near Your sacred House,” then: “that they may establish the prayer,” that is: I settled them to fulfill Your right, not to seek their portions. He was content that they be in the shades of His providence rather than in the shades of His favor. And he added: “Make the hearts of some people incline toward them” — that they may occupy themselves with Your worship, and that You may provide for my people, as long as they last, by Your sufficiency. For whoever stands in God's right, God stands by the right of his people.

mots.racine: س ك ن / ذ ر ر
mots.sens: I settled part of my offspring

mots.racine: و د ي / ز ر ع
ب ي ت / ح ر م

mots.ar: فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ
mots.tr: fa-jʿal afʾidatan mina n-nāsi tahwī ilayhim
mots.racine: ج ع ل / ف أ د / ه و ي
ر ز ق / ث م ر

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ مَا نُخْفِي وَمَا نُعْلِنُ ۗ وَمَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ
versets.tr: rabbanā innaka taʿlamu mā nukhfī wa-mā nuʿlinu wa-mā yakhfā ʿalā llāhi min shayʾin fī l-arḍi wa-lā fī s-samāʾi
versets.trad: Our Lord! You know what we conceal and what we proclaim. Nothing is hidden from God on earth or in the heaven.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: You have reserved for Yourself the knowledge of the unseen; no known thing escapes You; my state is not veiled from You — it is as You have known; You know my secret and my open. And whoever has known this simple sentence rests from the tremors of the Others, and his heart is relieved of the translations of thoughts and the dispersion in events coming from the Others. To know that God knows all unbinds from the anxious gaze on the created.

mots.racine: ع ل م / خ ف ي / ع ل ن
mots.sens: You know what we hide and show
mots.def: akhfā / aʿlana: to hide / to disclose.

mots.ar: وَمَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ
mots.tr: wa-mā yakhfā ʿalā llāhi min shayʾ
mots.racine: خ ف ي

versets.ar: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي وَهَبَ لِي عَلَى الْكِبَرِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ وَإِسْحَاقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّي لَسَمِيعُ الدُّعَاءِ
versets.tr: al-ḥamdu lillāhi lladhī wahaba lī ʿalā l-kibari ismāʿīla wa-isḥāqa inna rabbī la-samīʿu d-duʿāʾi
versets.trad: Praise be to God, who has granted me, in old age, Ishmael and Isaac! Truly my Lord hears the invocation.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He made him happy by the gift of a child in old age — which is akin to a miracle; therefore he praised God for it. And since this word comes after his request, in which he mentioned what preceded of His favors, glory to Him, and of His honoring by His lights, it has the sense of a delicate flattery: it is calling forth a new favor by an acknowledged favor. As if he said: as You have honored me with the gift of a child in old age, honor me with these things I have requested.

mots.racine: و ه ب / ك ب ر
mots.sens: He has given me in old age
mots.def: wahaba: to give as a gift.

mots.racine: س م ع / د ع و

versets.ar: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَاءِ
versets.tr: rabbi-jʿalnī muqīma ṣ-ṣalāti wa-min dhurriyyatī rabbanā wa-taqabbal duʿāʾi
versets.trad: My Lord! Make me one who establishes the prayer, and from my offspring also. Our Lord, accept my invocation!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in “make me the establisher of the prayer” an attestation: this indicates that the servants' acts are created. It means: create for me a prayer — for “making” and “creating” here have the same sense. When He makes someone the establisher of prayer, this amounts to making him a prayer. And of the offspring: make among them people who pray — for God elsewhere said: “My covenant does not reach the wrongdoers.”

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ق و م / ص ل و
mots.sens: make me the establisher of prayer

mots.racine: ذ ر ر
ق ب ل / د ع و

versets.ar: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْحِسَابُ
versets.tr: rabbanā-ghfir lī wa-li-wālidayya wa-lil-muʾminīna yawma yaqūmu l-ḥisābu
versets.trad: Our Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day of Reckoning.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the answering of invocation is, originally, a favor of His. It is not fitting for the servant to rely on another's invocation, however high his dignity; he must rather hang his heart upon God. For there is no more perfect invocation than Abraham's, nor more perfect concern than his concerning his father — and yet it did not avail and God did not intercede for him. And he adds: it is not fitting for the servant to cease his invocation out of fear it will not be answered; Abraham invoked for his parents without being answered, yet did not cease invoking.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / و ل د
أ م ن
ي و م / ق و م / ح س ب

versets.ar: وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللَّهَ غَافِلًا عَمَّا يَعْمَلُ الظَّالِمُونَ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ تَشْخَصُ فِيهِ الْأَبْصَارُ
versets.tr: wa-lā taḥsabanna llāha ghāfilan ʿammā yaʿmalu ẓ-ẓālimūna innamā yuʾakhkhiruhum li-yawmin tashkhaṣu fīhi l-abṣāru
versets.trad: Do not think God is unmindful of what the wrongdoers do. He only defers them to a Day when the eyes will stare in horror,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: threat for the wrongdoers, consolation for the oppressed. The oppressed, when he realizes that He, glory to Him, knows what he endures, the trial lightens in his heart and he becomes a bearer of suffering. And wrongdoing (ẓulm) has its declensions: wrong against the soul by putting the slip in place of obedience; wrong against the heart by letting malign thoughts settle in it; wrong against the spirit by destining it to love of creatures.

mots.racine: ح س ب / غ ف ل
أ خ ر / ي و م
mots.sens: He only defers them to a Day

mots.racine: ش خ ص / ب ص ر

versets.ar: مُهْطِعِينَ مُقْنِعِي رُءُوسِهِمْ لَا يَرْتَدُّ إِلَيْهِمْ طَرْفُهُمْ ۖ وَأَفْئِدَتُهُمْ هَوَاءٌ
versets.tr: muhṭiʿīna muqniʿī ruʾūsihim lā yartaddu ilayhim ṭarfuhum wa-afʾidatuhum hawāʾun
versets.trad: rushing forward, heads upraised, eyes unable to return to themselves, and hearts empty as air.
versets.commentaire: Verse of dread: they rush forward, heads raised, gazes fixed. Qushayrī (linking it to the preceding) distinguishes three degrees: this is for the common of believers — He has suspended their hearts on the deferred vengeance against the wrongdoers. But the elect, when they know that He, glory to Him, knows them in their state, pardon and are content with that. And the elect of the elect, knowing they are His servants, are not content with pardon for what was done to them of injustice until they ask forgiveness for the guilty — as the Prophet said: “My God, forgive my people, for they do not know.” The heart of the just even becomes intercessor.

mots.racine: ه ط ع / ق ن ع / ر أ س
ر د د / ط ر ف
mots.sens: their gaze does not return to them

mots.racine: ف أ د / ه و ي
mots.sens: and their hearts empty as air

versets.ar: وَأَنْذِرِ النَّاسَ يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمُ الْعَذَابُ فَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا رَبَّنَا أَخِّرْنَا إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ نُجِبْ دَعْوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ الرُّسُلَ ۗ أَوَلَمْ تَكُونُوا أَقْسَمْتُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ مَا لَكُمْ مِنْ زَوَالٍ
versets.tr: wa-andhiri n-nāsa yawma yaʾtīhimu l-ʿadhābu fa-yaqūlu lladhīna ẓalamū rabbanā akhkhirnā ilā ajalin qarībin nujib daʿwataka wa-nattabiʿi r-rusula a-wa-lam takūnū aqsamtum min qablu mā lakum min zawālin
versets.trad: Warn mankind of the Day when the punishment will reach them, and the wrongdoers will say: “Our Lord! Grant us a short respite, that we may answer Your call and follow the messengers.” Did you not swear before that you would never decline,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they spoiled the beginning of their affairs, failed in the duty incumbent upon them, and the flaw of their states has no more repair, nor their excuse any reception, so that the proof is established against them. The criminal among them is unmasked; the disbeliever is defeated; and the judgment against them is verified. To ask for respite at the threshold of the Day is to ask to push back the Day itself: none can.

mots.racine: ن ذ ر / ن و س
ي و م / أ ت ي / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: the Day the punishment comes to them

mots.racine: أ خ ر / أ ج ل / ق ر ب
mots.sens: defer us to a near term

mots.racine: ز و ل

versets.ar: وَسَكَنْتُمْ فِي مَسَاكِنِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ كَيْفَ فَعَلْنَا بِهِمْ وَضَرَبْنَا لَكُمُ الْأَمْثَالَ
versets.tr: wa-sakantum fī masākini lladhīna ẓalamū anfusahum wa-tabayyana lakum kayfa faʿalnā bihim wa-ḍarabnā lakumu l-amthāla
versets.trad: and dwelt in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, while it had been made clear to you how We dealt with them, and We had struck for you the parables?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We made the punishment fall upon them, and We made you see it — you saw it and drew no lesson from it; you followed their way, did as they did, and let yourselves be lulled by Our delay. So await from Us the treatment you have earned for what you have advanced. The frequentation of the people of passion and perversity and their neighborhood is a participation in their act; whoever frequents them thus receives what they receive; whoever follows them sinks in the fall, like them, to the bottom of his ruin.

mots.ar: وَسَكَنْتُمْ فِي مَسَاكِنِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ
mots.tr: wa-sakantum fī masākini lladhīna ẓalamū anfusahum
mots.racine: س ك ن / ظ ل م / ن ف س
mots.sens: you dwelt in the dwellings of the self-wronging

mots.racine: ب ي ن / ف ع ل
mots.sens: it became clear to you how We dealt

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.sens: We struck the parables for you

versets.ar: وَقَدْ مَكَرُوا مَكْرَهُمْ وَعِنْدَ اللَّهِ مَكْرُهُمْ وَإِنْ كَانَ مَكْرُهُمْ لِتَزُولَ مِنْهُ الْجِبَالُ
versets.tr: wa-qad makarū makrahum wa-ʿinda llāhi makruhum wa-in kāna makruhum li-tazūla minhu l-jibālu
versets.trad: They have plotted their plot, but with God is their plotting — though their plot was such as to move mountains.
versets.commentaire: Human stratagem is seized at its source: with God is their plot. Even were their schemes sufficient to shake the mountains, they do not escape His hand. The schemer schemes only within the decree of the One who sees the scheme before it forms.

mots.racine: م ك ر
ع ن د / م ك ر
ز و ل / ج ب ل

versets.ar: فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللَّهَ مُخْلِفَ وَعْدِهِ رُسُلَهُ ۗ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ ذُو انتِقَامٍ
versets.tr: fa-lā taḥsabanna llāha mukhlifa waʿdihi rusulahu inna llāha ʿazīzun dhū ntiqāmin
versets.trad: Think not that God will fail in His promise to His messengers. God is Mighty, Lord of retribution.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He does not fail His word given to His messengers, for He does not contradict the promise by the veracity of His word; and He has the power to punish them according to what He has promised them, by His right in His sovereignty. He is “Mighty,” none reaches Him, even a close friend; “Lord of retribution,” none escapes Him.

mots.ar: فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ اللَّهَ مُخْلِفَ وَعْدِهِ رُسُلَهُ
mots.tr: fa-lā taḥsabanna llāha mukhlifa waʿdihi rusulahu
mots.racine: ح س ب / خ ل ف / و ع د / ر س ل
mots.sens: do not think God breaks His promise to His messengers

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ن ق م
mots.sens: God is Mighty, Lord of retribution

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ الْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ الْأَرْضِ وَالسَّمَاوَاتُ ۖ وَبَرَزُوا لِلَّهِ الْوَاحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ
versets.tr: yawma tubaddalu l-arḍu ghayra l-arḍi wa-s-samāwātu wa-barazū lillāhi l-wāḥidi l-qahhāri
versets.trad: On the Day when the earth shall be changed into another earth, and the heavens likewise, and all shall appear before God, the One, the Subduer.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī brings the cosmic transformation back to inner experience: it is not the essence that changes, but the form — as when the stars are extinguished and the sky is split, one says it is not their essence that is changed, but times and places that change for men according to the difference of their states. Whoever passes from ease to trial says: “the times have changed”; likewise whoever passes from trial to ease. And in this order, the aspirant to whom comes a fall or weariness — the sun becomes for him uncovering, the earth quakes beneath him, the day becomes night, and the night becomes woe. The earth changed is the world traversed by a new state.

mots.racine: ي و م / ب د ل / أ ر ض
mots.sens: the Day the earth is changed into another

mots.racine: ب ر ز / و ح د / ق ه ر
mots.sens: they appear before God, the One, the Subduer
mots.def: al-Wāḥid: the One; al-Qahhār: the Subduer.

versets.ar: وَتَرَى الْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُقَرَّنِينَ فِي الْأَصْفَادِ
versets.tr: wa-tarā l-mujrimīna yawmaʾidhin muqarranīna fī l-aṣfādi
versets.trad: You shall see the guilty that Day bound together in fetters,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the aṣfād are the shackles. The fetters gather them, the chains bind them, pitch is their garment, boiling water their drink, the fire envelops them — such is the recompense of one who contradicted his God.

mots.racine: ج ر م
ق ر ن / ص ف د

versets.ar: سَرَابِيلُهُمْ مِنْ قَطِرَانٍ وَتَغْشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ النَّارُ
versets.tr: sarābīluhum min qaṭirānin wa-taghshā wujūhahumu n-nāru
versets.trad: their garments of pitch, and the fire covering their faces,
versets.commentaire: Immediate continuation of the preceding verse: Qushayrī includes it in the tableau of punishment. Pitch ignites and clings; the fire envelops the face, where arrogance and the averted gaze were lodged. The retribution takes the inverted form of the fault.

mots.racine: س ر ب ل / ق ط ر
غ ش و / و ج ه / ن و ر

versets.ar: لِيَجْزِيَ اللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ مَا كَسَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسَابِ
versets.tr: li-yajziya llāhu kulla nafsin mā kasabat inna llāha sarīʿu l-ḥisābi
versets.trad: that God may recompense each soul for what it has earned. Indeed God is swift in reckoning.
versets.commentaire: Each soul receives according to its acquisition (mā kasabat) — the work acquired by choice becomes the lot. And “God is swift in reckoning”: His knowledge embraces the totality instantly, without successive calculation. Where the human counts one by one, the Real sees all at a single glance.

mots.ar: لِيَجْزِيَ اللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ مَا كَسَبَتْ
mots.tr: li-yajziya llāhu kulla nafsin mā kasabat
mots.racine: ج ز ي / ن ف س / ك س ب
mots.sens: that God recompense each soul for what it earned
mots.def: jazā: to recompense; kasaba: to acquire.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ح س ب

versets.ar: هَٰذَا بَلَاغٌ لِلنَّاسِ وَلِيُنْذَرُوا بِهِ وَلِيَعْلَمُوا أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَٰهٌ وَاحِدٌ وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ أُولُو الْأَلْبَابِ
versets.tr: hādhā balāghun lin-nāsi wa-li-yundharū bihi wa-li-yaʿlamū annamā huwa ilāhun wāḥidun wa-li-yadhdhakkara ulū l-albābi
versets.trad: This is a Message for mankind, that they may be warned by it, that they may know He is One God, and that the people of understanding may take heed.
versets.commentaire: Seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: the proofs are manifest, the signs are in evidence, the calls are clear, the respite is ample, the Messenger — peace be upon him — is a deliverer, and the empowerment to fulfill the right of obligation is a helper. But the apportionment is prior, and the success to accomplish is sometimes refused. The Lord, glory to Him, does what He wills: whoever takes the lesson is saved, whoever forgets falls. To God belongs the command before and after — and God knows best.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ن و س
mots.sens: this is a message for mankind

mots.racine: ن ذ ر
mots.sens: that they may be warned by it

mots.racine: ع ل م / أ ل ه / و ح د
mots.sens: that they know He is One God

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ل ب ب
mots.sens: that the people of understanding may take heed
mots.def: ulū l-albāb: those of essential intelligence.
